history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin crashes on JDK 11 with IllegalAccessError due to classloader isolation when loading newer recipe jars.
history.qwen_rationale: Running the initial recipe step on JDK 17 avoids the JDK 11 module/classloader visibility restrictions that trigger the IllegalAccessError, allowing the Lombok version bump to apply successfully before continuing the migration.

history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin failed on JDK 17 with an IllegalAccessError inside its own Guava migration recipes due to Maven classloader isolation, preventing recipe execution.
history.qwen_rationale: Reverting the first step to JDK 11 aligns with the default template and avoids the JDK 17 classloader bug in the rewrite-maven-plugin. The subsequent steps follow the standard 11->17->21 progression.

history.qwen_observation: The first step fails during recipe application due to an IllegalAccessError in the rewrite-maven-plugin's classloader, likely caused by JDK 11 reflection restrictions and plugin version incompatibilities.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching the initial lombok bump to a POM_PATCH step bypasses the faulty mvn rewrite:run execution entirely for this step, allowing the build to proceed. Subsequent steps are shifted to JDK 17 to avoid JDK 11 reflection warnings that exacerbate the plugin's classloader issues.

history.qwen_observation: IllegalAccessError in NoGuavaImmutableListCopyOf during UpgradePluginsForJava17 due to Maven plugin classloader isolation on JDK 17.
history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradePluginsForJava17 meta-recipe triggers a classloader conflict with bundled Guava migration recipes. Removing it (and UpgradePluginsForJava21) avoids the crash, as UpgradeBuildToJava17/21 reliably handle the necessary maven-compiler-plugin and maven-surefire-plugin version bumps without pulling in the problematic Guava recipes.

history.qwen_observation: rewrite-maven-plugin crashes with IllegalAccessError on JDK 17+ due to classloader isolation issues with Guava migration recipes, and lombok_safe_bump fails on both JDK 11 and 17.
history.qwen_rationale: Switch lombok_safe_bump to a POM_PATCH step to bypass the broken mvn rewrite:run plugin entirely. Remove upgrade_plugins_for_java17/21 to avoid triggering the Guava classloader crash. Keep UpgradeBuildToJava17/21 and source transforms as RECIPE steps, relying on the plugin's core functionality which is less prone to the Guava-specific classloader bug.
